description_long: This is a dark, stealth-inspired airplane with a wide, sharply swept delta wing configuration and a compact, streamlined fuselage. The wings are integrated smoothly into the body, giving it a futuristic, almost bat-like appearance, with subtle surface paneling and vent details visible across the upper surfaces. The nose is pointed and tapers into a slightly bulbous cockpit area, which appears to be enclosed and seamlessly blended into the fuselage. There are no visible external engines or propellers, suggesting an internal jet propulsion system. The tail section is minimal, lacking a traditional vertical stabilizer, and the undercarriage is not visible, implying retractable landing gear. The overall finish is matte black with subtle textural variations, enhancing its stealthy, high-tech look.
description: A matte black, delta-wing airplane with a streamlined, stealthy design and an enclosed cockpit, featuring integrated wings and minimal tail structure.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, wide delta wings, and the smoothly integrated cockpit area.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlights the sleek, low-profile fuselage and the sharply swept wings blending into the body.
description_view_2: Top-down view reveals the full span of the delta wings and the symmetrical, bat-like planform.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizes the wing integration and the subtle surface detailing on the upper surfaces.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept delta wing with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles; wings are smoothly blended into the fuselage.
attribute_engine_type: No external engines visible; likely internal jet propulsion with no visible nacelles or intakes.
attribute_tail_design: Tailless configuration with no vertical stabilizer; the rear fuselage tapers smoothly without distinct tail surfaces.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable configuration integrated into the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit seamlessly integrated into the fuselage, likely single-seat with a smooth canopy.
